borrowed: Accessible-tree snapshots as the primary semantic proof for queue, task, recovery, and peer-route regions
Role and name stability as a release-facing hardening signal
rejected: Treating aria snapshots as a substitute for keyboard, reflow, or read-only fencing proof

borrowed: Deterministic screenshot baselines for calm, stale, read-only, large-queue, and reduced-motion states
rejected: Pixel-diff-only acceptance without semantic or writability checks

borrowed: Trace bundles as the primary debugging artifact for hardening failures
Step-level DOM and screenshot evidence for performance and focus regressions
rejected: Treating traces as optional once screenshots exist

borrowed: Isolated browser contexts for one-writer versus many-readers proof
rejected: Mocking secondary actors instead of proving fenced posture in separate contexts

borrowed: Cross-browser semantic proof across Chromium and a non-Chromium engine
rejected: Expanding browser coverage beyond the core paths at the cost of signal

borrowed: 320 CSS pixel-equivalent narrow-shell checks and no-horizontal-overflow enforcement
rejected: Single-breakpoint responsiveness claims without equivalent narrow and mission-stack proof

borrowed: Visible focus restoration after dialog dismissal and skip-link use
rejected: Low-contrast or outline-free focus treatments in dense professional surfaces

borrowed: 44px minimum click and tap affordances for dense but high-risk actions
rejected: Inflating every control uniformly and losing queue density

borrowed: Assistive announcement checks for queue churn, recovery, and degraded same-shell posture
rejected: Toast-only or visually silent state changes

borrowed: Interaction latency as supporting evidence for queue, command-palette, and task-transition responsiveness
rejected: Using Core Web Vitals alone as the release gate instead of shell-specific budgets

borrowed: Interaction-budget framing around main-thread responsiveness instead of just initial load
rejected: General-purpose web-app guidance that ignores same-shell clinical workflow continuity

borrowed: Calmer chrome, denser scan lines, and a stronger main-content hierarchy
rejected: Issue-tracker-specific framing, branding, and product navigation patterns

borrowed: Dense row grammar and stable position cues under responsive change
rejected: Toolbar-heavy table interactions in place of the current queue and task surfaces

borrowed: Plain, service-grade wording in the assurance lab and state summaries
rejected: Marketing-style explanatory copy in regulated operational surfaces

borrowed: Clear heading hierarchy and restrained emphasis in dense release-readiness boards
rejected: Literal reuse of NHS branding or public-service layout composition
